description: Unilever's Dove launched the 'Campaign for Real Beauty' in 2004, using non-professional models to challenge traditional beauty standards. It was praised for its social impact but critiqued for perceived corporate hypocrisy.

key_events.description: Billboards of 'real' women appear in major cities causing media stir.
Viral evolution series launched on early YouTube gaining millions of views.
Critics publish articles questioning if Dove parent company sells beauty products that promote insecurity.
Brand sentiment stabilizes as the campaign becomes an industry standard for 'purpose-led marketing'.

calibration_notes: Useful for calibrating long-term brand equity and the shift from niche controversy to mainstream acceptance.
